cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure out this square dance problem. So there are five dancers: Alice, Bob, Claire, Dave, and Eve. Each has a partner at the start. The question is about tracking their partner swaps through several steps and figuring out who Bob ends up with at the end. Let me take it step by step. First, I need to list out the initial pairings. The problem says: - Alice is with Lola - Bob is with Karl - Claire is with Patrick - Dave is with Sam - Eve is with Helga So initially, the partners are: Alice - Lola Bob - Karl Claire - Patrick Dave - Sam Eve - Helga Now, the problem says there are several partner swaps. Let me go through each one in order. First swap: Dave and Alice switch partners. So, Dave was with Sam, and Alice was with Lola. After switching, Dave would be with Lola, and Alice would be with Sam. So after first swap: Alice - Sam (previously Dave's partner) Dave - Lola (previously Alice's partner) Others remain the same: Bob - Karl Claire - Patrick Eve - Helga Second swap: Eve and Alice switch partners. Eve is currently with Helga, and Alice is with Sam. After swapping, Eve will take Alice's partner (Sam), and Alice will take Eve's partner (Helga). So after second swap: Alice - Helga Eve - Sam Others: Bob - Karl Claire - Patrick Dave - Lola Third swap: Bob and Dave switch partners. Bob is currently with Karl, Dave is with Lola. After swapping: Bob - Lola Dave - Karl Others: Alice - Helga Claire - Patrick Eve - Sam Fourth swap: Bob and Eve switch partners. Bob is now with Lola, Eve is with Sam. After swapping: Bob - Sam Eve - Lola Others: Alice - Helga Claire - Patrick Dave - Karl Fifth swap: Bob and Claire switch partners. Bob is with Sam, Claire is with Patrick. After swapping: Bob - Patrick Claire - Sam Others: Alice - Helga Dave - Karl Eve - Lola So, after all these swaps, the final pairings are: Alice - Helga Bob - Patrick Claire - Sam Dave - Karl Eve - Lola But wait, the question asks: At the end of the dance, Bob is dancing with ___? According to my final pairings, Bob is with Patrick. But looking at the options, option C is Patrick. However, let me double-check each step to make sure I didn't make a mistake. Let me go through each swap again. Initial pairings: Alice - Lola Bob - Karl Claire - Patrick Dave - Sam Eve - Helga First swap: Dave and Alice switch partners. So Dave (Sam) swaps with Alice (Lola). Now: Alice - Sam Dave - Lola Second swap: Eve (Helga) and Alice (Sam) swap. Alice - Helga Eve - Sam Third swap: Bob (Karl) and Dave (Lola) swap. Bob - Lola Dave - Karl Fourth swap: Bob (Lola) and Eve (Sam) swap. Bob - Sam Eve - Lola Fifth swap: Bob (Sam) and Claire (Patrick) swap. Bob - Patrick Claire - Sam Yes, so Bob ends up with Patrick. Option C is Patrick. But wait, let me check the options again. Options: (A) Lola (B) Karl (C) Patrick (D) Sam (E) Helga So option C is Patrick, which is what I got. Therefore, the answer should be C. But let me check once more, because sometimes these problems can have a trick. Wait, but maybe I confused the direction of the swaps? When they say "Dave and Alice switch partners," does that mean they exchange their partners, or that they become partners? The problem says "trade partners," so I think it's the former. For example, if Dave and Alice switch partners, then Dave takes Alice's previous partner and vice versa. Which is how I did it. Let me verify with another approach. Maybe using variables. Let me track Bob's partner throughout each step. Initially, Bob is with Karl. So Bob's partner starts as Karl. First swap: Dave and Alice switch partners. This doesn't involve Bob, so Bob's partner remains Karl. Second swap: Eve and Alice switch partners. Still no Bob, so partner remains Karl. Third swap: Bob and Dave switch partners. Before this swap, Bob has Karl, Dave has Lola. After swap, Bob's partner becomes Lola, Dave's becomes Karl. Fourth swap: Bob and Eve switch partners. Before this swap, Bob has Lola, Eve has Sam. After swap, Bob's partner becomes Sam, Eve's becomes Lola. Fifth swap:
